tex:
**[2020 October/November, Paper 33, Question 1]**

(a) A cruise ship travels 2067 km.
\newline

(i) (b) Write 2067 in words.
\newline

(c) Write 2067 correct to the nearest hundred.
\newline

(d) When full, the cruise ship carries 880 guests and 360 crew.
\newline

Write the ratio guests : crew in its simplest form.
\newline

 : 

(e) There are 480 cabins on the ship.
\newline
On one cruise, 456 of these cabins were used.
\newline

Find the percentage of cabins that were used.
\newline

 \%

(f) Last year the cost of a cruise was \$4600.
\newline
This year the cost of the same cruise is \$4784.
\newline

Work out the percentage increase in the cost.
\newline

 \%

(g) The cost of building the ship was \$153 000 000.
\newline

Write 153 000 000 in standard form.
\newline

(h) There are 480 cabins on the ship.
\newline
There are four types of cabin: Ocean-view, Balcony, Interior and Suite.
\newline
Hannah starts to draw a pie chart to show the numbers of each type of cabin.
\newline

(i) (i) Show that there are 120 Ocean-view cabins on the ship.
\newline

(j) The table shows information about each type of cabin.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{l l l}
Type of cabin & Number of cabins & Sector angle in a pie chart \\
Ocean-view & 120 & 90° \\
Balcony & 192 & 144° \\
Interior & 68 & \\
Suite & 100 & \\
\end{tabular}

(a) Complete the table.
\newline

(b) Complete the pie chart.
\newline

\newline

qtype: number properties (multiples, factors, primes, squares, odd, prime factors)
tex:
**[2020 October/November, Paper 33, Question 2]**

(a) Using numbers from 55 to 85, write down

(i) (b) a multiple of 23,

(c) a factor of 120,

(d) a common multiple of 8 and 12,

(e) a number that is both square and odd,

(f) a number that has exactly 2 factors.
\newline

(g) Write 220 as the product of its prime factors.
\newline

content.content: Using numbers from 55 to 85, write down
a multiple of 23,
a factor of 120,
a common multiple of 8 and 12,
a number that is both square and odd,

a number that has exactly 2 factors.
\newline

Write 220 as the product of its prime factors.
\newline

qtype: completing table of values and drawing quadratic graph
tex:
**[2020 October/November, Paper 33, Question 3]**

(a) Complete the table of values for $y = 1 + 5x - x^2$.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{c c c c c c c}
$x$ & -1 & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 \\
$y$ &  & 1 & 5 &  & 7 &  \\
\end{tabular}

(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 1 + 5x - x^2$ for $-1 \leq x \leq 5$.
\newline

(c) On the grid, draw the line $y = 3$.
\newline

Use your line to solve the equation $1 + 5x - x^2 = 3$.
\newline

$x =$ ...\ or $x =$ ...

\newline

\newline

tex:
**[2020 October/November, Paper 33, Question 4]**

(a) The diagram shows the plan of part of Rachel's garden.
\newline

Calculate the area.
\newline

(b) Rachel has a pond in her garden in the shape of a circle.
\newline
The circumference of the pond is 4.25 m.
\newline

Calculate the diameter of the pond.
\newline
Give your answer in centimetres.
\newline

(c) A plant pot is a cylinder with radius 15 cm and height 24 cm.
\newline

Calculate the volume of the pot.
\newline

(d) The diagram shows two mathematically similar plant pots.
\newline

The smaller pot has height 21.6 cm and diameter 27 cm.
\newline
The larger pot has diameter 33 cm.
\newline

Find the height, $h$, of the larger pot.
\newline

(e) A shop sells bags of compost in three different sizes.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{l l l}
Small & 30 litres & \$5.82 \\
Medium & 50 litres & \$9.45 \\
Large & 75 litres & \$14.50 \\
\end{tabular}

Work out which size of bag gives the best value.
\newline
Show how you decide.
\newline

content.content:
Rachel has a pond in her garden in the shape of a circle.
\newline
The circumference of the pond is 4.25 m.
\newline

Calculate the diameter of the pond.
\newline
Give your answer in centimetres.
\newline

A plant pot is a cylinder with radius 15 cm and height 24 cm.
\newline

Calculate the volume of the pot.
\newline

A shop sells bags of compost in three different sizes.
\newline

Work out which size of bag gives the best value.
\newline
Show how you decide.
\newline

tex:
The table shows the maximum power, kW, and the time taken, in seconds, to accelerate from 0 to $100\,\text{km/h}$ for each of 10 cars.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Maximum power (kW) & 77 & 52 & 103 & 55 & 44 & 51 & 85 & 135 & 90 & 110 \\
\hline
Time (seconds) & 12.5 & 14.9 & 9.0 & 12.1 & 14.4 & 12.9 & 10.0 & 7.1 & 11.0 & 9.4 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) (i)  Find the range of the times.
\newline

 Find the median maximum power.
\newline

(b) (i)  Complete the scatter diagram.
\newline
\\
The first eight points have been plotted for you.
\newline

 What type of correlation is shown on the scatter diagram?

 Describe the relationship between the maximum power of a car and the time taken to accelerate from 0 to $100\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

 Draw a line of best fit on the scatter diagram.
\newline

 Another car has a maximum power of 63$\,$kW.
\newline
\\
Use your line of best fit to estimate the time taken for this car to accelerate from 0 to $100\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

(c) Robert buys a car for \$$18\,160$.
\newline
\\
He pays a deposit of \$6460.
\newline
\\
He pays the rest of the money in 24 equal monthly payments.
\newline

Work out the amount of each monthly payment.
\newline

(d) A fuel tank holds 52 litres when full.
\newline
\\
The tank is a quarter full.
\newline
\\
Jim fills the tank with fuel that costs \$2.18 per litre.
\newline

Work out how much Jim pays.
\newline

tex:
**[2020 October/November, Paper 33, Question 6]**

(a) On the grid, draw the image of triangle A after:
(i) a translation by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} 3 \\ -7 \end{pmatrix}$, [2]
(ii) a reflection in the line $x = 3$.
\newline
 [2]

(iii) Describe fully the single transformation that maps triangle A onto triangle B.
\newline
 [3]

(b) $\mathbf{a} = \begin{pmatrix} 3 \\ -2 \end{pmatrix}$, $\mathbf{b} = \begin{pmatrix} 5 \\ 7 \end{pmatrix}$, $\mathbf{c} = \begin{pmatrix} -1 \\ 4 \end{pmatrix}$.
\newline

(i) $\mathbf{a} + \mathbf{b}$ [1]
(ii) $\mathbf{b} - 2\mathbf{c}$ [2]

(c) Point P has coordinates (6, -2) and PQ = $\begin{pmatrix} -4 \\ 5 \end{pmatrix}$.
\newline
Find the coordinates of point Q.
\newline

(, )
